chapter_title: Articles of stone, plaster, cement, asbestos, mica or similar materials
inclusions: Heading 68.01 — Setts, curbstones and flagstones, of natural stone (except slate)

Heading 68.02 — Worked monumental or building stone (except slate) and articles thereof, other than goods of heading 68.01; mosaic cubes and the like, of natural stone (including slate); artificially coloured granules, chippings and powder, of natural stone (including slate). Note 2: 'worked monumental or building stone' covers all natural stone worked similarly (quartzite, flint, dolomite, steatite, etc.) — but NOT slate
Heading 68.03 — Worked slate and articles of slate or of agglomerated slate

Heading 68.04 — Millstones, grindstones, grinding wheels and the like, without frameworks, for grinding, sharpening, polishing, trueing or cutting, hand sharpening or polishing stones, and parts thereof, of natural stone, of agglomerated natural or artificial abrasives, or of ceramics, with or without parts of other materials

Heading 68.05 — Natural or artificial abrasive powder or grain, on a support of textile material, of paper, of paperboard or of other materials, whether or not cut to shape or sewn or otherwise made up

Heading 68.06 — Slag wool, rock wool and similar mineral wools; exfoliated vermiculite, expanded clays, foamed slag and similar expanded mineral materials; mixtures and articles of heat-insulating, sound-insulating or sound-absorbing mineral materials, other than those of heading 68.11 or 68.12 or of Chapter 69
Heading 68.07 — Articles of asphalt or of similar material (for example, petroleum bitumen or coal tar pitch)

Heading 68.08 — Panels, boards, tiles, blocks and similar articles of vegetable fibre, of straw or of shavings, chips, particles, sawdust or other waste, of wood, agglomerated with cement, plaster or other mineral binders
Heading 68.09 — Articles of plaster or of compositions based on plaster: boards, sheets, panels, tiles and similar articles, not ornamented; other articles
Heading 68.10 — Articles of cement, of concrete or of artificial stone, whether or not reinforced
Heading 68.11 — Articles of asbestos-cement, of cellulose fibre-cement or the like

Heading 68.12 — Fabricated asbestos fibres; mixtures with a basis of asbestos or with a basis of asbestos and magnesium carbonate; articles of such mixtures or of asbestos (e.g., thread, woven fabric, clothing, headgear, footwear, gaskets), whether or not reinforced, other than goods of heading 68.11 or 68.13

Heading 68.13 — Friction material and articles thereof (for example, sheets, rolls, strips, segments, discs, washers, pads), not mounted, for brakes, for clutches or the like, with a basis of asbestos, of other mineral substances or of cellulose, whether or not combined with textile or other materials
Heading 68.14 — Worked mica and articles of mica, including agglomerated or reconstituted mica, whether or not on a support of paper, paperboard or other materials
Heading 68.15 — Articles of stone or of other mineral substances (including carbon fibres, articles of carbon fibres and articles of peat), not elsewhere specified or included

exclusions.condition: Natural stone, rock, sand, gravel, mineral earths in raw/unworked primary form
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 25 (salt, sulphur, earths and stone) — Note 1(a). Raw quarried stone → Ch.25. Worked/manufactured stone articles → Ch.68

exclusions.condition: Paper or paperboard coated, impregnated or covered with mica powder, graphite, bitumen or asphalt
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 48.10 or 48.11 (coated paper and paperboard) — Note 1(b). The paper substrate dominates classification

exclusions.condition: Textile fabric coated or covered with mica powder, bitumen or asphalt
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 56 or Chapter 59 (coated/impregnated textile fabrics) — Note 1(c). The textile substrate dominates

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 71 (jewellery, precious stones, imitation jewellery made from stone or mineral)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 71 — Note 1(d). Gemstones → Ch.71. Ornamental mineral stones as jewellery → Ch.71

exclusions.condition: Tools or parts of tools of Chapter 82, including those with stone/abrasive working parts
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 82 — Note 1(e). Diamond-tipped drill bits → 82.07. Stone cutting blades for machines → 82.08. Exception: millstones, grindstones and grinding wheels → 68.04 (NOT Ch.82 — heading 68.04 is explicitly in Ch.68)
Redirect to heading 84.42 (printing machinery type-setting machines) — Note 1(f). Lithographic stones prepared for printing use → 84.42, not 68.02

exclusions.condition: Electrical insulators of stone/mineral material (heading 85.46) or fittings of insulating material (heading 85.47)
exclusions.action: Redirect to 85.46 or 85.47 — Note 1(g). Stone/ceramic electrical insulators → 85.46 (see also Ch.69 Note 2(f))

exclusions.condition: Dental burrs of stone or abrasive material
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 90.18 (instruments used in medical/dental/veterinary sciences) — Note 1(h). Dental drill burrs → 90.18 regardless of abrasive material

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Scope of 'worked monumental or building stone' in heading 68.02
classification_rules.description: Heading 68.02 covers 'worked monumental or building stone' from ALL natural stone types — not just the specific stones in headings 25.15 (marble, travertine, alabaster) or 25.16 (granite, porphyry, basalt, sandstone) but also quartzite, flint, dolomite, steatite and ALL other natural stone. ONE EXCEPTION: slate is ALWAYS excluded from 68.02 — worked slate → heading 68.03. Cutting to shape, polishing, drilling, moulding etc. = 'worked' for 68.02 purposes.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.25 (raw/primary mineral forms) vs Ch.68 (articles of stone/mineral)
classification_rules.description: The raw vs. worked distinction is fundamental: Ch.25 = natural stones, earths, minerals in raw, crushed, or minimally processed PRIMARY FORM (rough blocks, cut slabs, crushed stone, gravel, sand). Ch.68 = stone and mineral ARTICLES that have been further worked/manufactured (polished monuments, tiles, paving, building stone articles, abrasive products). A marble block rough-cut from quarry → Ch.25. A polished marble slab for flooring → 68.02. A marble tile cut to size → 68.02.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 68.04 (millstones/grinding wheels) vs 68.05 (coated abrasives on backing) vs 68.13 (friction materials)
classification_rules.description: Three headings for abrasive/friction products of stone or mineral: 68.04 = GRINDING/SHARPENING WHEELS and stones — rigid shaped abrasive tools (millstones, grinding wheels, hand sharpening stones) — natural stone, agglomerated abrasives or ceramics. 68.05 = abrasive powder or grain ON A FLEXIBLE BACKING (sandpaper, emery cloth, abrasive paper/fabric/other materials). 68.13 = FRICTION MATERIAL (brake pads, clutch linings) — not for cutting/grinding but for friction braking/clutching, with asbestos or other mineral basis. The intended function (grinding/sharpening vs. sanding/polishing vs. friction braking) separates the headings.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 68.06 (mineral wools/expanded minerals) vs 70.19 (glass wool) vs Ch.69 (ceramic insulation)
classification_rules.description: Heading 68.06 covers MINERAL WOOLS that do NOT meet the glass wool definition of Ch.70 Note 4: rock wool (high basalt/diabase content, low SiO2), slag wool, stone wool → 68.06. Glass wool (SiO2 ≥60%, or SiO2 <60% with alkaline oxide >5% or B2O3 >2%) → 70.19. Fired ceramic insulation products → Ch.69. Also in 68.06: expanded minerals (vermiculite, expanded clay, foamed slag) and mixes of mineral insulation materials not of Ch.69 or 68.11/68.12.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 68.09 (plaster articles) vs 68.10 (cement/concrete articles) vs 68.11 (asbestos-cement) vs 68.08 (wood-mineral composite boards)
classification_rules.description: Four headings for building material compositions: 68.08 = boards/panels of VEGETABLE FIBRE/WOOD WASTE + mineral binder (cement, plaster, gypsum — e.g., wood-cement boards, particle board with mineral binder). 68.09 = articles of PLASTER or plaster-based compositions (gypsum boards, plasterboard, ornate plaster articles). 68.10 = articles of CEMENT, CONCRETE or ARTIFICIAL STONE (concrete blocks, precast concrete panels, terrazzo tiles, concrete pipes). 68.11 = articles of ASBESTOS-CEMENT specifically or cellulose fibre-cement. The binding agent determines the heading.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 68.15 (other stone/mineral articles NES) vs Ch.69 (fired ceramic articles) vs Ch.38 (carbon fibres/articles with specific heading)
classification_rules.description: Heading 68.15 is the catch-all for stone and mineral substance articles not described in any other heading of Ch.68 or other chapters. Explicitly included in 68.15: CARBON FIBRES, articles of carbon fibres, and articles of PEAT. Articles of fired ceramic → Ch.69 (not 68.15). Articles of asphalt → 68.07. Articles of graphite for electrical use → 85.45. Carbon fibres and carbon fibre articles (including reinforced composites) → 68.15 unless more specifically described elsewhere.
